design_philosophy.core_loop: Spin wheel, solve letter-guessing puzzle, accumulate winnings across rounds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk/reward between spinning for high values vs. solving incomplete puzzles; letter deduction under time/resource constraints
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Participate in the iconic Wheel of Fortune game show experience; test vocabulary and deduction skills
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Faithful translation of game show format rather than pure puzzle game; prioritizes show authenticity over gameplay innovation. Conservative prize structure ($1,000 cap) reflects daytime show era constraints. Puzzle selection and wedge variety suggest intentional scoping decisions.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Puzzle difficulty varies by puzzle bank; player controls which letters to guess and wheel-spinning strategy. Limited agency in formal difficulty selection implied by game show format adherence.

progression.upgrade_system: Accumulating cash/winnings across rounds; potential prize multipliers based on wheel wedges
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through predetermined puzzle bank; three-round structure mirrors show format
replayability_hooks: Large puzzle bank with unused puzzles suggesting extensive content
Wheel wedge variety enables different difficulty/risk scenarios per playthrough
Multiple game show rounds create replay appeal

world_building_techniques: Game show setting/framing (visual presentation, puzzle categories)
Brand licensing and show authenticity (Vanna White association implied by series)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Wheel of Fortune NES page)

metadata_notes.source_limitations: Available source focuses on development artifacts (unused content, revisions) rather than design documentation. Core gameplay loop, difficulty mechanics, and narrative delivery inferred from game show format and genre conventions. Boss design not applicable to puzzle game paradigm.
high_confidence_assertions: Show fidelity as primary design driver
